response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web developer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role across its most critical axes. The job requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is an absolute prerequisite — the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond the language dealbreaker, the role demands deep C programming for embedded UNIX/Linux firmware, network-switch security features (MACsec, 802.1X), and Linux kernel modification experience; Bogdan's C exposure is minimal and dated (a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015), with the vast majority of his career spent on TypeScript, Node.js, and web-platform work. There is no evidence of security engineering, Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, switch chip knowledge, or embedded firmware development.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) demonstrating adaptability across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Linux is listed as a core skill and he has worked in Linux environments throughout his career
Some low-level systems exposure: C/C++ for NI LabWindows, DLang for high-performance async I/O, and bachelor thesis work involving Linux/JACK audio and C++
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
Primary skill set is web/cloud (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, React, AWS), with no demonstrated security engineering, network-switch, or embedded-firmware experience
C programming is marginal and over a decade old; no Linux kernel development, kernel vulnerability research, or chip-vendor SDK experience is evident
